input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML (ItemsPage.xaml)**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage"
             x:DataType="vm:ItemsViewModel">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <CollectionView.Header>
            <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                         IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
            </RefreshView>
        </CollectionView.Header>

    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

Actually, `RefreshView` wraps the `CollectionView` — here's the correct structure:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="vm:ItemModel">
                <VerticalStackLayout Padding="12">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="18" />
                    <Label Text="{Binding Description}" FontSize="14"
                           TextColor="Gray" />
                </VerticalStackLayout>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>

</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel (ItemsViewModel.cs)**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<ItemModel> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ItemModel? _selectedItem;

    public ItemsViewModel(IApiService api) => _api = api;

    // Fires automatically when SelectedItem changes
    partial void OnSelectedItemChanged(ItemModel? value)
    {
        if (value is null) return;
        Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = value });
        SelectedItem = null; // reset so the same item can be re-selected
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<ItemModel>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false; // stops the spinner
        }
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"` | Drives the list from an `ObservableCollection` |
| `SelectionMode="Single"` | Allows exactly one item selected at a time |
| `SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"` | Two-way; the generated `OnSelectedItemChanged` partial method triggers navigation |
| `RefreshView.Command` | Calls `RefreshCommand` (generated from `[RelayCommand]` on `RefreshAsync`) |
| `IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"` | Controls the pull-to-refresh spinner; set `false` when done |

**Don't forget** to set `SelectedItem = null` after navigating — otherwise tapping the same item a second time won't fire the property change.
